illness_description_by_patient_conflicti: When I walk uphill I feel pressure in my chest, though I also notice burning after late meals. Yesterday I felt sweaty and the pain moved to my jaw. I’m 61 and have a smoker with high cholesterol.

Radiation to the arm or jaw and associated sweating are classic high-risk clues for myocardial ischemia. The reflux-like features, such as burning after spicy food and partial response to antacids, are real conflicting clues, but they are lower-specificity findings and do not neutralize the cardiac risk pattern. The patient’s age and vascular risk factors increase the pretest probability of acute coronary syndrome. A safer and more coherent interpretation is that this is unstable angina or another ACS presentation until proven otherwise.
